seoTitle: How to Use Zendesk for Customer Service Day-to-Day
metaDescription: A practical overview of how to use Zendesk for customer service, covering views, macros, ticket fields, and performance monitoring for daily operations.
intro: New teams adopting Zendesk often need a general walkthrough of daily operations rather than a single feature deep dive. This guide covers the core workflow: organizing incoming tickets, replying consistently with macros, updating ticket fields, and checking team performance.

steps.heading: Organize incoming queues
steps.body: Build and sort personalized views in the sidebar based on priority levels, brands, and open states so nothing slips through.

steps.heading: Open and respond to tickets
steps.body: Click into a ticket to launch the unified conversation canvas, which merges email, messaging, chat, and voice into one window.

steps.heading: Standardize responses with macros
steps.body: Search and apply pre-written responses using the macros shortcut panel to save time on repetitive answers.

steps.heading: Update ticket fields
steps.body: Adjust urgency, category type, and alphanumeric tags on each ticket to keep your data clean and reportable.

steps.heading: Move tickets through their lifecycle
steps.body: Match customer-facing status dropdowns to Open, Pending, or Solved as the case progresses.

steps.heading: Check performance
steps.body: Open Reporting to audit active performance rows and monitor agent capacity and resolution trends.

faq.question: Is Zendesk only for email support?
faq.answer: No, the Agent Workspace consolidates email, messaging, live chat, and voice into a single interface.

faq.question: How do macros help daily support work?
faq.answer: Macros let agents insert pre-written text and automatically update ticket fields in one click, cutting down repetitive typing.

faq.question: What's the difference between a view and a macro?
faq.answer: A view filters and organizes which tickets you see, while a macro applies actions and text to an individual ticket.

faq.question: Can I see how my team is performing in real time?
faq.answer: Yes, real-time monitoring dashboards in the Reporting section track agent capacity and resolution trends.
